know.d: To know means to have information or understanding about something.
know.e: I know how to ride a bike.

keep.d: To have and hold onto something
keep.e: I need to keep my room clean.

kind.e: She was very kind to the new student.

known.d: something you are sure is true
known.e: It is known that the sun rises in the east every morning.

king.d: A man who rules a country
king.e: The king of the castle sat on his throne.

killed.d: To cause someone or something to die
killed.e: The tornado killed many trees in the park.

kids.e: The kids are playing outside in the yard.

knew.d: To have information or understanding about something
knew.e: I knew the answer to the question.

key.d: A small metal tool used to open or lock something.
key.e: The boy used the key to unlock the door.

knowledge.d: What you know and have learned
knowledge.e: The student has a lot of knowledge about science

knows.d: To have information or understanding about something
knows.e: My teacher knows a lot about history.

kept.d: to continue having or holding something
kept.e: I kept my favorite toy on my bed.

kid.e: The kid was very excited to go to the park.

killing.d: To cause the death of a living thing
killing.e: Hunting is a way to get food, but killing animals should be done carefully.

keeping.d: Continuing to have or hold something.
keeping.e: The zookeeper is responsible for keeping the animals safe.

knowing.d: Being aware of information or understanding something.
knowing.e: I have a knowing look on my face when I solve a puzzle.

kick.d: To push or hit something with your foot.
kick.e: I can kick the ball really far.

kingdom.d: A country or land ruled by a king or queen.
kingdom.e: The kingdom celebrated the king's birthday.

km.d: a way to measure distance that is longer than a mile
km.e: The park is 5 km away from our house.

kitchen.d: The room in a house where cooking is done
kitchen.e: My mom is in the kitchen making a delicious meal.

keeps.d: to continue to have or hold something
keeps.e: The dog keeps a close eye on his food bowl so no one takes it.

korea.d: a country in Asia with a rich culture and delicious food
korea.e: My friend's family is from Korea and they taught me how to make kimchi.

kinda.e: The weather is kinda cold today

kiss.d: When you touch someone's lips with your own
kiss.e: The prince gave the princess a gentle kiss

kinds.d: Different types or groups of things.
kinds.e: There are many kinds of animals in the zoo.

kevin.e: Kevin likes to play baseball with me.

kong.d: a large, famous ape character in stories and movies
kong.e: King Kong was a mighty and powerful creature.
